name: BedJet
description: The `fan` platform exposes the BedJet's fan-related functionality, including on/off and speed control.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the fan. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the fan in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Restore Mode
config_entries.description: Control how the fan attempts to restore state on boot.

options.label: Always initialize the fan as ON on bootup.
Attempt to restore state and default to OFF if not possible to restore.
Attempt to restore state and default to ON.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to OFF.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to ON.

config_entries.label: Bedjet ID
config_entries.description: The ID of the Bedjet component.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Direction Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive fan direction commands on (options: forward, reverse, toggle).

config_entries.label: Direction State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish fan direction state changes to (options: forward, reverse).

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Oscillation Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive oscillation commands on.

config_entries.label: Oscillation State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish fan oscillation state changes to.

config_entries.label: Speed Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive speed commands on (options: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH).

config_entries.label: Speed Level Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive numeric speed commands on (range: 0 to speed count).

config_entries.label: Speed Level State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish numeric fan speed state changes to (range: 0 to speed count).

config_entries.label: Speed State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish fan speed state changes to (options: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH).
